facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
facts.targetLanguageWord: とは比べものにならない
facts.reading: とはくらべものにならない
facts.pronunciation: to wa kurabe mono ni naranai

facts.quizQuestion:
そんな簡単なこと、説明する{___}。
(There's no need to explain something so simple.)
facts.explanation: までもない (no need to) — そんな簡単なこと、説明するまでもない。The action is unnecessary because it is obvious.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
言う{___}、彼は天才だ。
(It goes without saying that he is a genius.)
facts.explanation: までもない (no need to) — 言うまでもない、彼は天才だ。Classic phrase meaning 'needless to say'.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
確認する{___}、答えは明らかだ。
(There's no need to confirm — the answer is obvious.)
facts.explanation: までもない (no need to) — 確認するまでもない、答えは明らかだ. Action is superfluous given how obvious the situation is.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
わざわざ来てもらう{___}、私が持って行く。
(There's no need to have you come — I'll bring it.)
facts.explanation: までもない (no need to) — わざわざ来てもらうまでもない、私が持って行く。The effort is unnecessary.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
条件次第では、引き受け{___}。
(Depending on the conditions, it's not impossible for me to take it on.)
facts.explanation: ないものでもない (not impossible) — 条件次第では、引き受けないものでもない。Expresses reluctant possibility.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
頑張れば、合格でき{___}と思う。
(If I try hard, I think it's not impossible to pass.)
facts.explanation: ないものでもない (not impossible) — 頑張れば、合格できないものでもないと思う。Cautious optimism.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この提案は、改善すれば認め{___}。
(This proposal, if improved, is not impossible to approve.)
facts.explanation: ないものでもない (not impossible) — この提案は、改善すれば認めないものでもない。Conditional possibility.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の言い訳も、理解でき{___}。
(His excuse is not impossible to understand.)
facts.explanation: ないものでもない (not impossible) — 彼の言い訳も、理解できないものでもない。Grudging understanding.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女はプロ{___}の腕前を持つアマチュア選手だ。
(She is an amateur player with skills on par with professionals.)
facts.explanation: 並み (on par with/of the same level as) — 彼女はプロ並みの腕前を持つアマチュア選手だ。Indicates reaching the same standard as the referenced group.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その子供は大人{___}の知識を持っている。
(That child has knowledge on par with an adult.)
facts.explanation: 並み (on par with) — その子供は大人並みの知識を持っている。Child surpassing expected level.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の英語力は母語話者{___}だ。
(His English proficiency is on par with native speakers.)
facts.explanation: 並み (on par with) — 彼の英語力は母語話者並みだ。High praise for language ability.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は世界{___}の技術を持つ職人だ。
(She is an artisan with world-class skills.)
facts.explanation: 並み (on par with/of the level of) — 彼女は世界並みの技術を持つ職人だ。World-class standard.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
今年は去年{___}暑い夏になった。
(This year became an even hotter summer than last year.)
facts.explanation: にもまして (more than/even more than) — 今年は去年にもまして暑い夏になった。Degree exceeds a previous reference point.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は以前{___}熱心に勉強するようになった。
(He came to study even more enthusiastically than before.)
facts.explanation: にもまして (even more than) — 彼は以前にもまして熱心に勉強するようになった。Surpassing a previous state.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
何{___}、健康が大切だ。
(More than anything else, health is important.)
facts.explanation: にもまして (more than/above all) — 何にもまして、健康が大切だ。Placing something above all else in priority.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
昨日{___}、今日はたくさんのお客が来た。
(Even more than yesterday, many customers came today.)
facts.explanation: にもまして (even more than) — 昨日にもまして、今日はたくさんのお客が来た。Direct temporal comparison.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この問題は日本{___}、世界中に影響している。
(This issue affects not only Japan but the entire world.)
facts.explanation: にとどまらず (not limited to/extending beyond) — この問題は日本にとどまらず、世界中に影響している。Scope extends beyond the initial reference.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の名声は国内{___}、海外でも知られている。
(His fame is known not only domestically but also abroad.)
facts.explanation: にとどまらず (not limited to) — 彼の名声は国内にとどまらず、海外でも知られている。Fame or influence extending beyond expected boundaries.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その映画は若者{___}、中高年にも人気がある。
(That movie is popular not only among young people but also the middle-aged and elderly.)
facts.explanation: にとどまらず (not limited to) — その映画は若者にとどまらず、中高年にも人気がある。Wide demographic appeal.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このサービスは無料{___}、多くの特典も提供している。
(This service is not limited to being free — it also provides many benefits.)
facts.explanation: にとどまらず (not limited to) — このサービスは無料にとどまらず、多くの特典も提供している。Scope exceeds the already-good feature.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
起こる{___}起きた事故だ。
(It was an accident that happened as it was destined to happen.)
facts.explanation: べくして (as destined to/naturally) — 起こるべくして起きた事故だ. The event was predictable; an inevitable outcome of the conditions.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
勝つ{___}勝ったチームだ。
(It is a team that won as it was destined to win.)
facts.explanation: べくして (as destined to) — 勝つべくして勝ったチームだ. The victory was the natural result of superior preparation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
失敗する{___}失敗した計画だった。
(It was a plan that failed as it was destined to fail.)
facts.explanation: べくして (as destined to) — 失敗するべくして失敗した計画だった. Hindsight shows the failure was built in from the start.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
出会う{___}出会った二人だと思う。
(I think they are two people who met as they were destined to meet.)
facts.explanation: べくして (as destined to) — 出会うべくして出会った二人だ. Romantic framing of fate.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
こんなに人を思いやる行動が愛情{___}。
(If such a considerate action is not love, then what is it?)
facts.explanation: でなくてなんだろう (what else could it be but) — 愛情でなくてなんだろう. Rhetorical question asserting that the label is the only possible one.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
命を賭けて人を救う行為が英雄{___}。
(If risking one's life to save a person is not heroism, what is it?)
facts.explanation: でなくてなんだろう (what else but) — 英雄でなくてなんだろう. Rhetorical force asserts an obvious conclusion.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これほどの苦しみが試練{___}。
(If suffering this much is not a trial, what is it?)
facts.explanation: でなくてなんだろう (what else but) — 試練でなくてなんだろう. Reframes unbearable suffering as purposeful challenge.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
40年間同じ店を守り続けたことが情熱{___}。
(If protecting the same shop for 40 years is not passion, what is it?)
facts.explanation: でなくてなんだろう (what else but) — 情熱でなくてなんだろう. Long dedication reframed as undeniable proof of passion.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これこそが真の勇気という者{___}。
(Could this not be what is truly called courage?)
facts.explanation: ではあるまいか (isn't it/could it not be — rhetorical, formal) — 真の勇気という者ではあるまいか. Formal rhetorical question leading the reader to a conclusion.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この問題こそが、現代社会が直面すべき最大の課題{___}。
(Could this problem not be the greatest challenge modern society must face?)
facts.explanation: ではあるまいか (isn't it) — 最大の課題ではあるまいか. Formal rhetorical assertion common in essays and speeches.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これこそが人類に残された最後のチャンス{___}。
(Could this not be humanity's last remaining chance?)
facts.explanation: ではあるまいか (could it not be) — 最後のチャンスではあるまいか. Urgent rhetorical question in formal writing.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
今こそ行動すべき時{___}。
(Could this not be the time when we should act?)
facts.explanation: ではあるまいか (isn't it) — 行動すべき時ではあるまいか. Formal call to action framed as rhetorical question.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
頑張った{___}、こんな素晴らしい結果になった。
(Because there was worth in working hard, such a wonderful result came about.)
facts.explanation: 甲斐がある (worth doing/it pays off) — 頑張った甲斐がある. Confirms that effort produced meaningful reward.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
教えた{___}、彼はどんどん上手くなっている。
(There is worth in teaching him — he is getting better and better.)
facts.explanation: 甲斐がある (worth it) — 教えた甲斐がある. The student's progress justifies the teacher's effort.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
毎日練習した{___}、試合に勝てた。
(There was worth in practising every day — I was able to win the match.)
facts.explanation: 甲斐がある (worth it/paying off) — 毎日練習した甲斐がある。Success vindicates the daily effort.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
苦労し{___}、こんなに成長した。
(There was worth in suffering hardship — look how much I have grown.)
facts.explanation: 甲斐がある (worth it) — 苦労した甲斐がある。Hardship is reframed as investment with visible returns.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
必死の看護の{___}、患者は亡くなった。
(Despite desperate nursing, to no avail, the patient passed away.)
facts.explanation: 甲斐もなく (to no avail/despite one's efforts) — 必死の看護の甲斐もなく、患者は亡くなった. Expresses grief that effort was ultimately futile.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
説得を試みた{___}、彼は聞き入れなかった。
(Despite attempting persuasion, to no avail, he did not listen.)
facts.explanation: 甲斐もなく (to no avail) — 説得を試みた甲斐もなく、彼は聞き入れなかった. Effort was expended but produced no result.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
全力を尽くした{___}、試合に負けてしまった。
(Despite giving everything, to no avail, we lost the match.)
facts.explanation: 甲斐もなく (to no avail) — 全力を尽くした甲斐もなく、試合に負けてしまった. Disappointment when maximum effort still fails.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
長年の研究の{___}、成果は出なかった。
(Despite years of research, to no avail, no results emerged.)
facts.explanation: 甲斐もなく (to no avail) — 長年の研究の甲斐もなく、成果は出なかった. Long-term investment yielding nothing.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あの人の態度は失礼{___}。
(That person's attitude is extremely rude.)
facts.explanation: 極まりない/極まる (extremely/to the extreme degree) — 失礼極まりない. Formal intensifier attached to nouns or な-adjective stems.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
そのような発言は危険{___}行為だ。
(Such a statement is an extremely dangerous act.)
facts.explanation: 極まりない (extremely) — 危険極まりない行為だ. Used in formal or written style to stress an extreme negative quality.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このような不衛生な環境は不快{___}。
(Such an unsanitary environment is extremely unpleasant.)
facts.explanation: 極まりない (extremely) — 不快極まりない. Expresses maximum degree of displeasure in formal style.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あの政治家の行動は不誠実{___}。
(That politician's behaviour is extremely dishonest.)
facts.explanation: 極まりない (extremely) — 不誠実極まりない. Political critique delivered in formal register.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたのご支援は、ありがたい{___}。
(Your support is the utmost of gratitude.)
facts.explanation: ことこの上ない (utmost/nothing more ... than) — ありがたいことこの上ない. Formal superlative expressing that nothing exceeds this degree.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
二人が無事に帰ってきたことは、嬉しい{___}。
(The fact that both returned safely is the utmost of happiness.)
facts.explanation: ことこの上ない (utmost) — 嬉しいことこの上ない. Formal expression of joy at its peak.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
そのような対応は、失礼{___}。
(Such a response is the utmost of rudeness.)
facts.explanation: ことこの上ない (utmost/supreme) — 失礼ことこの上ない. Can be used for negative extremes as well as positive.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このような機会をいただけるとは、光栄{___}。
(To be given such an opportunity is the utmost of honour.)
facts.explanation: ことこの上ない (utmost) — 光栄ことこの上ない. Used in formal speeches and written acknowledgements.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
借金{___}、あんな高級車を買う必要はない。
(There is no need to buy such a luxury car even to the point of going into debt.)
facts.explanation: までして (even to the point of doing) — 借金までして、あんな高級車を買う必要はない. Criticises an extreme measure taken for an unworthy goal.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
体を壊す{___}、働き続けるのはよくない。
(It is not good to keep working even to the point of ruining one's health.)
facts.explanation: までして (even to the point of) — 体を壊すまでして、働き続けるのはよくない. Questions whether the cost is worth the effort.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
人を傷つける{___}、自分が得をしたいとは思わない。
(I don't want to profit even to the point of hurting others.)
facts.explanation: までして (even to the point of) — 人を傷つけるまでして、自分が得をしたいとは思わない. States a moral limit the speaker won't cross.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
嘘をつく{___}、彼を守ろうとしたのだ。
(She tried to protect him even to the point of telling a lie.)
facts.explanation: までして (even to the point of) — 嘘をつくまでして、彼を守ろうとした. The extreme measure highlights the depth of devotion.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この映画は見た人を感動させ{___}。
(This film will definitely move everyone who sees it.)
facts.explanation: ないではおかない (will definitely/inevitably) — 感動させないではおかない. Something has such power that it will inevitably produce the effect.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の演技は観客を笑わせ{___}。
(His acting will definitely make the audience laugh.)
facts.explanation: ないではおかない (will definitely) — 笑わせないではおかない. The subject's power to produce a reaction is presented as irresistible.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その音楽は聴く人を涙ぐませ{___}。
(That music will definitely bring tears to those who hear it.)
facts.explanation: ないではおかない (will inevitably) — 涙ぐませないではおかない. Describes an emotionally irresistible piece of music.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この本はあなたの人生観を変え{___}。
(This book will definitely change your outlook on life.)
facts.explanation: ないではおかない (will definitely) — 変えないではおかない. The book's power to transform the reader is presented as certain.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
こんな大きなミスをしたのだから、謝ら{___}。
(Having made such a big mistake, you cannot get away without apologising.)
facts.explanation: ないではすまない (must inevitably/cannot get away without) — 謝らないではすまない. Social obligation; failure to act will be socially unacceptable.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これだけ迷惑をかけておいて、賠償し{___}。
(After causing this much trouble, you cannot get away without compensating.)
facts.explanation: ないではすまない (cannot get away without) — 賠償しないではすまない. Indicates that society or conscience demands the action.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
約束を破ったのだから、説明し{___}。
(Having broken a promise, you cannot get away without explaining.)
facts.explanation: ないではすまない (must inevitably) — 説明しないではすまない. The broken promise creates a social obligation to explain.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
人に怪我をさせたのだから、責任を取ら{___}。
(Having injured someone, you cannot get away without taking responsibility.)
facts.explanation: ないではすまない (must inevitably) — 責任を取らないではすまない. Moral and social accountability.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この成功は皆の努力の結果{___}。
(This success is nothing but the result of everyone's efforts.)
facts.explanation: にほかならない (nothing but/is precisely) — この成功は皆の努力の結果にほかならない. Asserts that the stated cause or identity is the sole explanation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼が辞めたのは、会社への不満{___}。
(The reason he quit is nothing but dissatisfaction with the company.)
facts.explanation: にほかならない (nothing but) — 会社への不満にほかならない. Pinpoints the one true cause.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
それはあなたへの愛情の表れ{___}。
(That is nothing but an expression of love for you.)
facts.explanation: にほかならない (nothing but) — 愛情の表れにほかならない. Identifies the deeper meaning behind an action.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の言葉は脅迫{___}。
(His words are nothing but a threat.)
facts.explanation: にほかならない (nothing but/is precisely) — 彼の言葉は脅迫にほかならない. Strips away ambiguity to name the true nature of something.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あんな無責任なことを言うなんて、失礼{___}。
(Saying such an irresponsible thing — there is a limit to rudeness.)
facts.explanation: にもほどがある (there is a limit to) — 失礼にもほどがある. Expresses strong indignation that something has gone too far.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
毎日遅刻するなんて、いい加減さ{___}。
(Being late every day — there is a limit to carelessness.)
facts.explanation: にもほどがある (there is a limit to) — いい加減さにもほどがある. Strong scolding; the behaviour has exceeded what is tolerable.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
そんな嘘をつくなんて、厚かましさ{___}。
(Telling such a lie — there is a limit to shamelessness.)
facts.explanation: にもほどがある (there is a limit to) — 厚かましさにもほどがある. Blunt expression of exasperation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あんな簡単な問題を間違えるなんて、うかつさ{___}。
(Making such a simple mistake — there is a limit to carelessness.)
facts.explanation: にもほどがある (there is a limit to) — うかつさにもほどがある. Criticises negligence with exasperated emphasis.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
子供から大人{___}、この映画は人気がある。
(From children to adults, this film is popular.)
facts.explanation: に至るまで (to the extent of/ranging all the way to) — 子供から大人に至るまで、この映画は人気がある. Indicates that the range extends even to the furthest point.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
細部{___}、彼は完璧を追求する。
(All the way to the finest details, he pursues perfection.)
facts.explanation: に至るまで (all the way to) — 細部に至るまで、彼は完璧を追求する. Emphasises thoroughness that leaves no corner unchecked.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
日常の食事から特別なイベント{___}、この食材は使われている。
(From everyday meals all the way to special events, this ingredient is used.)
facts.explanation: に至るまで (all the way to/ranging to) — 日常の食事から特別なイベントに至るまで、この食材は使われている. Spans the breadth of usage.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
過去から現在{___}、人類は戦争を繰り返してきた。
(From the past all the way to the present, humanity has repeated war.)
facts.explanation: に至るまで (all the way to) — 過去から現在に至るまで、人類は戦争を繰り返してきた. Temporal range emphasising continuity.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
溢れ{___}、彼女の目に涙が浮かんだ。
(As if about to overflow, tears welled up in her eyes.)
facts.explanation: んばかりに (as if about to) — 溢れんばかりに、彼女の目に涙が浮かんだ. Literary; describes something at the very point of occurrence.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
割れ{___}、観客の拍手が鳴り響いた。
(As if about to burst, the audience's applause rang out.)
facts.explanation: んばかりに (as if about to) — 割れんばかりに、観客の拍手が鳴り響いた. The applause is so intense it feels like it could break something.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
爆発せ{___}、彼は怒りをこらえた。
(As if about to explode, he held back his anger.)
facts.explanation: んばかりに (as if about to) — 爆発せんばかりに、彼は怒りをこらえた. Vivid description of suppressed rage.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
崩れ落ち{___}、彼は壁に寄りかかった。
(As if about to crumble and fall, he leaned against the wall.)
facts.explanation: んばかりに (as if about to) — 崩れ落ちんばかりに、彼は壁に寄りかかった. Literary image of exhaustion to the point of collapse.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
若気{___}とはいえ、あの行為は反省している。
(Although it was the folly of youth, I reflect on that action.)
facts.explanation: の至り (utmost/extreme — formal set phrase) — 若気の至り means 'youthful indiscretion / the folly of youth'. Fixed formal phrase.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このような栄誉を賜り、光栄{___}でございます。
(To be bestowed with such an honour is the utmost of glory.)
facts.explanation: の至り (the utmost — formal/honorific) — 光栄の至りでございます. Extremely formal; used in speeches and written commendations.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あんな失礼なことを言ってしまい、赤面{___}です。
(Having said such a rude thing, I am at the utmost of embarrassment.)
facts.explanation: の至り (utmost) — 赤面の至りです. Formal and literary; admits profound shame.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
お褒めの言葉をいただき、恐縮{___}でございます。
(To receive such words of praise, I am at the utmost of humility.)
facts.explanation: の至り (utmost — formal) — 恐縮の至りでございます. Used to express humble gratitude in formal or ceremonial contexts.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このような栄誉をいただけるとは、光栄{___}です。
(To receive such an honour is the height of glory.)
facts.explanation: の極み (the height/utmost of — formal) — 光栄の極みです. Used in formal contexts to express the pinnacle of a feeling or state.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
こんな失礼なことを言われるとは、屈辱{___}だ。
(To be told such a rude thing is the height of humiliation.)
facts.explanation: の極み (the height of) — 屈辱の極みだ. Expresses the most extreme degree of a feeling.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この景色の美しさは、感動{___}だ。
(The beauty of this scenery is the height of emotion.)
facts.explanation: の極み (the utmost of) — 感動の極みだ. Elevates an aesthetic experience to its maximum intensity.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
こんな素晴らしい賞をいただけるとは、感謝{___}です。
(To receive such a wonderful award is the utmost of gratitude.)
facts.explanation: の極み (utmost — formal) — 感謝の極みです. Formal expression of profound gratitude.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
一秒{___}無駄にできない。
(I cannot waste even one second.)
facts.explanation: たりとも～ない (not even one) — 一秒たりとも無駄にできない. Expresses absolute zero tolerance for any waste, even the smallest unit.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
一円{___}払う気はない。
(I have no intention of paying even one yen.)
facts.explanation: たりとも～ない (not even one) — 一円たりとも払う気はない. Absolute refusal quantified at the smallest possible amount.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
一人{___}欠席は許されない。
(Not even one person's absence is permitted.)
facts.explanation: たりとも～ない (not even one) — 一人たりとも欠席は許されない. Strict rule with zero exceptions.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
一歩{___}退くつもりはない。
(I have no intention of retreating even one step.)
facts.explanation: たりとも～ない (not even one) — 一歩たりとも退くつもりはない. Military or debate metaphor expressing absolute resolve.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
指導者{___}、率先して行動すべきだ。
(Those who are leaders should take the initiative and act.)
facts.explanation: たるもの (those who are/for someone who is) — 指導者たるもの、率先して行動すべきだ. Formal; sets the standard expected of a role.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
社会人{___}、時間を守るのは基本だ。
(For someone who is a working adult, keeping to time is fundamental.)
facts.explanation: たるもの (for someone who is) — 社会人たるもの、時間を守るのは基本だ. Invokes the expectations attached to a social role.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
医師{___}、患者の命を最優先に考えなければならない。
(Those who are doctors must place the patient's life above all else.)
facts.explanation: たるもの (for those who are) — 医師たるもの、患者の命を最優先に考えなければならない. Formal ethical injunction addressed to a professional role.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
親{___}、子供の手本となるべきだ。
(Those who are parents should be a model for their children.)
facts.explanation: たるもの (for those who are) — 親たるもの、子供の手本となるべきだ. Sets an aspirational standard for the parental role.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これほど貢献した人には、もっと評価され{___}。
(Someone who has contributed this much should be more highly appreciated.)
facts.explanation: てしかるべきだ (should rightly be/it is appropriate that) — もっと評価されてしかるべきだ. Asserts that the outcome is only right and proper.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
こんな重大な問題は、議会で議論され{___}。
(Such a serious issue should rightly be debated in parliament.)
facts.explanation: てしかるべきだ (should rightly be) — 議会で議論されてしかるべきだ. Formal assertion of what proper procedure demands.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼のような才能は、もっと早く認められ{___}。
(Talent like his should rightly have been recognised much sooner.)
facts.explanation: てしかるべきだ (should rightly be) — 認められてしかるべきだ. Expresses regret that something proper was not done.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これだけの実績があれば、昇進し{___}。
(With a track record like this, being promoted would only be right.)
facts.explanation: てしかるべきだ (it is only right that) — 昇進してしかるべきだ. Argues that the promotion is warranted by the evidence.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女の幸せを願っ{___}。
(I ceaselessly wish for her happiness.)
facts.explanation: てやまない (ceaselessly/from the bottom of one's heart) — 彼女の幸せを願ってやまない. Formal; expresses an enduring and deep wish or feeling.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この国の発展を期待し{___}。
(I ceaselessly expect the development of this country.)
facts.explanation: てやまない (ceaselessly) — この国の発展を期待してやまない. Formal; used in speeches to express enduring hope.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
皆さんのご健康を祈っ{___}。
(I ceaselessly pray for everyone's good health.)
facts.explanation: てやまない (ceaselessly/from the bottom of one's heart) — 皆さんのご健康を祈ってやまない. Formal closing expression in speeches.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
亡き師の教えを尊敬し{___}。
(I ceaselessly respect the teachings of my late teacher.)
facts.explanation: てやまない (ceaselessly) — 亡き師の教えを尊敬してやまない. Formal expression of lasting veneration.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
待ってましたっ{___}、彼は手を挙げた。
(As if to say 'I've been waiting for this!', he raised his hand.)
facts.explanation: とばかりに (as if to say) — 待ってましたっとばかりに、彼は手を挙げた. Describes a vivid action that communicates a message without words.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
どうだっ{___}、彼女は優勝カップを高く掲げた。
(As if to say 'How about that!', she raised the championship cup high.)
facts.explanation: とばかりに (as if to say) — どうだっとばかりに、彼女は優勝カップを高く掲げた. The triumphant gesture expresses what words do not.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
早く終われっ{___}、上司は何度も時計を見た。
(As if to say 'Hurry up and finish!', the boss glanced at his watch repeatedly.)
facts.explanation: とばかりに (as if to say) — 早く終われっとばかりに、上司は何度も時計を見た. Non-verbal pressure communicated through action.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
ここが見せ場だっ{___}、彼は全力で演奏した。
(As if to say 'This is my moment!', he played with all his might.)
facts.explanation: とばかりに (as if to say) — ここが見せ場だっとばかりに、彼は全力で演奏した. The performer's attitude communicates his intent.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
親が留守なの{___}、子供たちは好き放題やった。
(Taking advantage of their parents being away, the children did whatever they liked.)
facts.explanation: をいいことに (taking advantage of) — 親が留守なのをいいことに、子供たちは好き放題やった. Exploiting a situation or loophole for one's own benefit.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
上司が出張中なの{___}、早退する社員が続出した。
(Taking advantage of the boss being on a business trip, employees started leaving early one after another.)
facts.explanation: をいいことに (taking advantage of) — 上司が出張中なのをいいことに、早退する社員が続出した. Opportunistic behaviour enabled by an absent authority.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
規制がない{___}、業者は好き勝手に工場を建てた。
(Taking advantage of there being no regulations, the companies built factories as they pleased.)
facts.explanation: をいいことに (taking advantage of) — 規制がないをいいことに、好き勝手に工場を建てた. Regulatory gap exploited for profit.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
先生が優しい{___}、生徒たちは授業中も騒いでいた。
(Taking advantage of the teacher being kind, the students made noise even during class.)
facts.explanation: をいいことに (taking advantage of) — 先生が優しいをいいことに、授業中も騒いでいた. Leniency exploited as an opportunity for misbehaviour.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
東京公演{___}、全国ツアーが始まった。
(Starting with the Tokyo performance, the nationwide tour began.)
facts.explanation: を皮切りに (starting with/kicking off with) — 東京公演を皮切りに、全国ツアーが始まった. Marks the first in a series of events.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この発見{___}、新たな研究が次々と始まった。
(Starting with this discovery, new research began one after another.)
facts.explanation: を皮切りに (starting with) — この発見を皮切りに、新たな研究が次々と始まった. The first event triggers a cascade of related developments.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
社長のスピーチ{___}、パーティーが始まった。
(Starting with the president's speech, the party began.)
facts.explanation: を皮切りに (starting with) — 社長のスピーチを皮切りに、パーティーが始まった. Marks the opening act that launches an event.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
一人の抗議{___}、大規模なデモへと発展した。
(Starting with one person's protest, it developed into a large-scale demonstration.)
facts.explanation: を皮切りに (starting with) — 一人の抗議を皮切りに、大規模なデモへと発展した. A single spark igniting a movement.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あの子供たちの笑顔を見て、感動{___}。
(Seeing those children's smiles, I cannot help but feel moved.)
facts.explanation: を禁じ得ない (cannot help but feel — formal) — 感動を禁じ得ない. Formal expression for an emotion that cannot be suppressed.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その悲惨な映像を見て、涙{___}。
(Seeing that tragic footage, I cannot help but shed tears.)
facts.explanation: を禁じ得ない (cannot help but) — 涙を禁じ得ない. Formal literary statement of irresistible emotion.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の無責任な態度に、怒り{___}。
(In the face of his irresponsible attitude, I cannot help but feel anger.)
facts.explanation: を禁じ得ない (cannot help but feel) — 怒りを禁じ得ない. Formal admission of an emotion triggered by someone else's conduct.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その美しい音楽に、感涙{___}。
(At that beautiful music, I cannot help but shed tears of emotion.)
facts.explanation: を禁じ得ない (cannot help but feel) — 感涙を禁じ得ない. Formal expression for being moved to tears by beauty.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この仕事ができるのは、彼女{___}。
(There is no one other than her who can do this job.)
facts.explanation: をおいてほかにない (there is none other than) — 彼女をおいてほかにない. Asserts someone or something as uniquely irreplaceable.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
今この国を救える指導者は彼{___}。
(There is no leader other than him who can save this country now.)
facts.explanation: をおいてほかにない (there is none other than) — 彼をおいてほかにない. Strong assertion of uniqueness in a critical moment.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この役を演じられる俳優は彼{___}。
(There is no actor other than him who can play this role.)
facts.explanation: をおいてほかにない (none other than) — 彼をおいてほかにない. Artistic context; the person is the one perfect fit.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この問題を解決できるのは、あなた{___}。
(There is no one other than you who can solve this problem.)
facts.explanation: をおいてほかにない (none other than) — あなたをおいてほかにない. A direct appeal to an individual's unique capability.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
台風の影響で、イベントは中止{___}。
(Due to the typhoon, the event was forced to be cancelled.)
facts.explanation: を余儀なくされる (be forced to/have no choice but to) — イベントは中止を余儀なくされた. Formal; describes a compelled or unavoidable action.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
経済的な理由から、彼は退学{___}。
(For financial reasons, he was forced to withdraw from school.)
facts.explanation: を余儀なくされる (be forced to) — 退学を余儀なくされた. Passive construction showing circumstances left no choice.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
震災の影響で、多くの住民が避難{___}。
(Due to the earthquake, many residents were forced to evacuate.)
facts.explanation: を余儀なくされる (be forced to) — 避難を余儀なくされた. Disaster context; emphasises involuntary displacement.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
会社の倒産により、従業員は解雇{___}。
(Due to the company's bankruptcy, the employees were forced to be dismissed.)
facts.explanation: を余儀なくされる (be forced to) — 解雇を余儀なくされた. Business crisis context; circumstances dictate the outcome.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
嵐{___}、彼らは山頂を目指した。
(Undaunted by the storm, they aimed for the summit.)
facts.explanation: をものともせず (undaunted by/defying) — 嵐をものともせず、彼らは山頂を目指した. Expresses heroic indifference to an obstacle.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
けが{___}、彼は試合に出場し続けた。
(Undaunted by his injury, he continued to compete.)
facts.explanation: をものともせず (undaunted by) — けがをものともせず、彼は試合に出場し続けた. Commends determination in the face of physical adversity.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
激しい批判{___}、彼女は改革を推し進めた。
(Undaunted by fierce criticism, she pushed forward with reform.)
facts.explanation: をものともせず (undaunted by) — 激しい批判をものともせず、彼女は改革を推し進めた. Courage in the face of social opposition.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
貧困{___}、彼は独学で医師になった。
(Undaunted by poverty, he became a doctor through self-study.)
facts.explanation: をものともせず (defying/undaunted by) — 貧困をものともせず、彼は独学で医師になった. Triumph over socioeconomic adversity.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
批判を覚悟の上で、{___}反対意見を述べた。
(Prepared for criticism, I dared to state an opposing opinion.)
facts.explanation: 敢えて (dare to/deliberately despite difficulty) — 批判を覚悟の上で、敢えて反対意見を述べた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
危険と知りながら、{___}その山に挑んだ。
(Knowing it was dangerous, he dared to challenge that mountain.)
facts.explanation: 敢えて (dare to/deliberately) — 危険と知りながら、敢えてその山に挑んだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は{___}困難なルートを選んだ。
(He deliberately chose the difficult route.)
facts.explanation: 敢えて (dare to/deliberately) — 彼は敢えて困難なルートを選んだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}言わせてもらうが、その案には根本的な問題がある。
(If I dare to say so, there is a fundamental problem with that proposal.)
facts.explanation: 敢えて (dare to) — 敢えて言わせてもらうが、その案には根本的な問題がある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は{___}自分の主張を譲らなかった。
(He persistently refused to give up his argument to the very end.)
facts.explanation: あくまでも (to the end/persistently/stubbornly) — 彼はあくまでも自分の主張を譲らなかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これは{___}個人的な意見であり、会社の立場を代表するものではない。
(This is strictly a personal opinion and does not represent the company's position.)
facts.explanation: あくまでも (strictly/to the end) — これはあくまでも個人的な意見であり、会社の立場を代表するものではない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は{___}原則に従って行動する人だ。
(She is a person who acts strictly in accordance with principles.)
facts.explanation: あくまでも (strictly/persistently) — 彼女はあくまでも原則に従って行動する人だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
交渉は難航したが、担当者は{___}妥協しなかった。
(Negotiations were difficult, but the person in charge stubbornly refused to compromise.)
facts.explanation: あくまでも (persistently/to the very end) — 交渉は難航したが、担当者はあくまでも妥協しなかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
心配していた通り、{___}試験に落ちてしまった。
(Just as I had feared, sure enough he failed the exam.)
facts.explanation: 案の定 (just as one thought/sure enough) — 心配していた通り、案の定試験に落ちてしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
天気予報の通り、{___}午後から雨が降り始めた。
(Just as the weather forecast said, sure enough it started raining in the afternoon.)
facts.explanation: 案の定 (sure enough/just as expected) — 天気予報の通り、案の定午後から雨が降り始めた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
無理な計画だと思っていたが、{___}頓挫した。
(I had thought it was an unreasonable plan, and sure enough it fell apart.)
facts.explanation: 案の定 (sure enough/just as one thought) — 無理な計画だと思っていたが、案の定頓挫した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼が遅刻すると予想していたが、{___}また遅れてきた。
(I expected him to be late, and sure enough he was late again.)
facts.explanation: 案の定 (sure enough/just as expected) — 彼が遅刻すると予想していたが、案の定また遅れてきた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
参加者は{___}席を予約しておく必要がある。
(Participants need to reserve their seats in advance.)
facts.explanation: あらかじめ (beforehand/in advance) — 参加者はあらかじめ席を予約しておく必要がある。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
緊急時に備え、{___}避難経路を確認しておいてください。
(Please confirm evacuation routes beforehand in preparation for emergencies.)
facts.explanation: あらかじめ (beforehand/in advance) — 緊急時に備え、あらかじめ避難経路を確認しておいてください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
手術前に{___}リスクについて説明を受けた。
(Before the surgery I was given an explanation about the risks in advance.)
facts.explanation: あらかじめ (beforehand/in advance) — 手術前にあらかじめリスクについて説明を受けた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
イベント当日に混雑が予想されるため、{___}オンラインで入場券を購入してください。
(As the event day is expected to be crowded, please purchase admission tickets online in advance.)
facts.explanation: あらかじめ (beforehand/in advance) — イベント当日に混雑が予想されるため、あらかじめオンラインで入場券を購入してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

acceptableAlternatives: いかんにかかっている
facts.quizQuestion:
結果は今後の努力{___}。
(The result depends on future effort.)
facts.explanation: いかんだ (depends on/formal) — 結果は今後の努力いかんだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

acceptableAlternatives: いかんにかかっている
facts.quizQuestion:
成否は準備の{___}。
(Whether it succeeds or fails depends on preparation.)
facts.explanation: いかんだ (depends on/formal) — 成否は準備のいかんだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
理由の{___}、規則は守らなければならない。
(Regardless of the reason, the rules must be followed.)
facts.explanation: いかんにかかわらず (regardless of/no matter what) — 理由のいかんにかかわらず、規則は守らなければならない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

acceptableAlternatives: いかんにかかっている
facts.quizQuestion:
プロジェクトが成功するかどうかは、チームワークの{___}。
(Whether the project succeeds depends on teamwork.)
facts.explanation: いかんだ (depends on/formal) — プロジェクトが成功するかどうかは、チームワークのいかんだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}この技術は世界中に広まるだろう。
(Eventually this technology will spread throughout the world.)
facts.explanation: いずれ (eventually/sooner or later) — いずれこの技術は世界中に広まるだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
今は難しいかもしれないが、{___}状況は改善するはずだ。
(It may be difficult now, but sooner or later the situation should improve.)
facts.explanation: いずれ (sooner or later/eventually) — 今は難しいかもしれないが、いずれ状況は改善するはずだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}にせよ、詳細はまた連絡します。
(In any case, I will be in touch about the details.)
facts.explanation: いずれにせよ (in any case/either way) — いずれにせよ、詳細はまた連絡します。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}自分でも試してみようと思っている。
(I am thinking of eventually trying it myself.)
facts.explanation: いずれ (eventually) — いずれ自分でも試してみようと思っている。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この街には{___}、賑やかな商店街があった。
(This town once had a lively shopping district.)
facts.explanation: かつて (once/formerly/in the past) — この街にはかつて、賑やかな商店街があった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は{___}、国際的に有名な音楽家だった。
(He was once an internationally famous musician.)
facts.explanation: かつて (once/formerly) — 彼はかつて、国際的に有名な音楽家だった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
{___}ないほど混んでいる地下鉄に乗った。
(I rode on a subway that was crowded like never before.)
facts.explanation: かつて (ever/before/in the past — in negative: never before) — かつてないほど混んでいる地下鉄に乗った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この建物は{___}、有名な作家が住んでいた家だ。
(This building is the house where a famous writer formerly lived.)
facts.explanation: かつて (formerly/once) — この建物はかつて、有名な作家が住んでいた家だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
事態は{___}取り返しのつかない状況になった。
(The situation has already become irreversible.)
facts.explanation: もはや (already/no longer — often preceding negative) — 事態はもはや取り返しのつかない状況になった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その技術は{___}時代遅れとなった。
(That technology has already become outdated.)
facts.explanation: もはや (already/by now) — その技術はもはや時代遅れとなった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼との関係は{___}修復不可能だ。
(The relationship with him is already beyond repair.)
facts.explanation: もはや (already/no longer) — 彼との関係はもはや修復不可能だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
子どもたちは{___}スマートフォンなしでは生きられない世代になった。
(Children have already become a generation that cannot live without smartphones.)
facts.explanation: もはや (already/by now) — 子どもたちはもはやスマートフォンなしでは生きられない世代になった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
他社{___}、この新技術を導入した。
(We introduced this new technology ahead of other companies.)
facts.explanation: に先駆けて (ahead of/pioneering before others) — 他社に先駆けて、この新技術を導入した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
発売{___}、限定プレビューが開催された。
(A limited preview was held ahead of the general release.)
facts.explanation: に先駆けて (ahead of/before) — 発売に先駆けて、限定プレビューが開催された。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
世界{___}、日本はその研究を開始した。
(Japan began the research ahead of the world.)
facts.explanation: に先駆けて (ahead of/pioneering) — 世界に先駆けて、日本はその研究を開始した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
開会式{___}、会場の設営が始まった。
(Setup of the venue began ahead of the opening ceremony.)
facts.explanation: に先駆けて (ahead of/before) — 開会式に先駆けて、会場の設営が始まった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
お越しの{___}、ぜひお声がけください。
(When you come, please do not hesitate to say hello.)
facts.explanation: 折に (when/at the time of — formal/literary) — お越しの折に、ぜひお声がけください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
機会の{___}ぜひご連絡ください。
(When the occasion arises, please do not hesitate to contact me.)
facts.explanation: 折に (when/at the time of) — 機会の折にぜひご連絡ください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
東京にお越しの{___}、ぜひ当社にお立ち寄りください。
(When you visit Tokyo, please do stop by our company.)
facts.explanation: 折に (when/at the time of — formal) — 東京にお越しの折に、ぜひ当社にお立ち寄りください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このような{___、先生にご挨拶するのが礼儀だ。
(On such an occasion, it is good manners to greet one's teacher.)
facts.explanation: 折に (on such an occasion) — このような折に、先生にご挨拶するのが礼儀だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
会場には{___}500名が集まった。
(About 500 people gathered at the venue.)
facts.explanation: およそ (about/approximately/roughly) — 会場にはおよそ500名が集まった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この工事は{___}三ヶ月かかる予定だ。
(This construction is expected to take approximately three months.)
facts.explanation: およそ (approximately/about) — この工事はおよそ三ヶ月かかる予定だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の言動は{___}常識とかけ離れていた。
(His behavior was utterly removed from common sense.)
facts.explanation: およそ (utterly/completely — used in negative contexts meaning 'not at all') — 彼の言動はおよそ常識とかけ離れていた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このプロジェクトには{___}1億円の費用がかかる見込みだ。
(This project is expected to cost approximately 100 million yen.)
facts.explanation: およそ (approximately/about) — このプロジェクトにはおよそ1億円の費用がかかる見込みだ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は{___}知っているかのように話した。
(He spoke as if he knew all about it.)
facts.explanation: さも (as if/very much so in an exaggerated way) — 彼はさも知っているかのように話した。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は{___}忙しそうな顔をしているが、実際は暇だ。
(She looks very much as if she is busy, but actually she has plenty of time.)
facts.explanation: さも (as if/very much so) — 彼女はさも忙しそうな顔をしているが、実際は暇だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
子供は{___}嬉しそうに、プレゼントの包みを開けた。
(The child opened the present wrapping looking very much as if overjoyed.)
facts.explanation: さも (as if/very much so) — 子供はさも嬉しそうに、プレゼントの包みを開けた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は{___}当然のように席に座った。
(He sat down in the seat as if it were perfectly natural.)
facts.explanation: さも (as if/very much so) — 彼はさも当然のように席に座った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
今すぐ謝りなさい。{___}、もう友達でいられない。
(Apologize right now. Otherwise, we can no longer be friends.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (otherwise/if not) — 今すぐ謝りなさい。さもないと、もう友達でいられない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
締め切りまでに提出してください。{___}、審査を受けられない。
(Please submit by the deadline. Otherwise, your application cannot be reviewed.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (otherwise/if not) — 締め切りまでに提出してください。さもないと、審査を受けられない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
薬を飲んでおきなさい。{___}、症状が悪化するよ。
(Take the medicine now. Otherwise, your symptoms will get worse.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (otherwise/if not) — 薬を飲んでおきなさい。さもないと、症状が悪化するよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
早く逃げなさい。{___}、巻き込まれるぞ。
(Run away now. Otherwise, you will get caught up in it.)
facts.explanation: さもないと (otherwise/if not) — 早く逃げなさい。さもないと、巻き込まれるぞ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
試験に合格したと聞いて、ご両親は{___}喜んでいることでしょう。
(Hearing that you passed the exam, your parents must surely be overjoyed.)
facts.explanation: さぞ (I imagine/surely must be) — 試験に合格したと聞いて、ご両親はさぞ喜んでいることでしょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あんな事故に遭ったのだから、{___}怖かったことだろう。
(Having been in such an accident, it must surely have been frightening.)
facts.explanation: さぞ (I imagine/surely must be) — あんな事故に遭ったのだから、さぞ怖かったことだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
長旅でお疲れでしょうから、{___}お腹も空いていることでしょう。
(You must be tired from the long trip, and I imagine you must surely be hungry too.)
facts.explanation: さぞ (I imagine/surely must be) — 長旅でお疲れでしょうから、さぞお腹も空いていることでしょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
初めて舞台に立った時、{___}緊張したことだろう。
(Standing on stage for the first time, you must surely have been very nervous.)
facts.explanation: さぞ (surely must have been) — 初めて舞台に立った時、さぞ緊張したことだろう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
本日{___}、閉会とさせていただきます。
(We hereby close the proceedings for today.)
facts.explanation: をもちまして (hereby/by means of — formal closing) — 本日をもちまして、閉会とさせていただきます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
以上{___}、株主総会の全議事を終了いたします。
(With this, we hereby conclude all proceedings of the shareholders' meeting.)
facts.explanation: をもちまして (hereby/by means of — formal) — 以上をもちまして、株主総会の全議事を終了いたします。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
今月末{___}、当店は閉店いたします。
(Our store will close hereby at the end of this month.)
facts.explanation: をもちまして (as of/hereby) — 今月末をもちまして、当店は閉店いたします。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この度{___}、弊社サービスの提供を終了いたします。
(We hereby announce the termination of our company's services.)
facts.explanation: をもちまして (hereby/by means of this) — この度をもちまして、弊社サービスの提供を終了いたします。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
誠意{___}、交渉に臨む所存です。
(I intend to approach the negotiations with sincerity.)
facts.explanation: をもって (by means of/with — formal) — 誠意をもって、交渉に臨む所存です。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このメール{___}、正式なご連絡とさせていただきます。
(We hereby use this email as official notification.)
facts.explanation: をもって (by means of/as — formal) — このメールをもって、正式なご連絡とさせていただきます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
全力{___}、この事業に取り組む。
(I will tackle this project with full effort.)
facts.explanation: をもって (by means of/with) — 全力をもって、この事業に取り組む。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
本日付け{___}退職いたします。
(I hereby resign as of today's date.)
facts.explanation: をもって (as of/hereby) — 本日付けをもって退職いたします。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
散歩し{___}、友人の家に立ち寄った。
(While taking a walk, I stopped by a friend's house.)
facts.explanation: がてら (while/on the occasion of) — 散歩しがてら、友人の家に立ち寄った。Doing something as an incidental secondary purpose.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
買い物し{___}、郵便局にも寄った。
(While shopping, I also stopped at the post office.)
facts.explanation: がてら (while/on the occasion of) — 買い物しがてら、郵便局にも寄った。Secondary action accomplished during the primary one.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
運動し{___}、公園の花を眺めた。
(While exercising, I gazed at the flowers in the park.)
facts.explanation: がてら (while/on the occasion of) — 運動しがてら、公園の花を眺めた。Combining purposes in one trip or activity.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
ドライブし{___}、温泉に立ち寄ることにした。
(While on a drive, we decided to stop by a hot spring.)
facts.explanation: がてら (while/on the occasion of) — ドライブしがてら、温泉に立ち寄ることにした。Typical casual travel context.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
お礼{___}、先生のお宅を訪問した。
(I visited my teacher's home both to express thanks and for another purpose.)
facts.explanation: かたがた (while/for dual purpose, formal) — お礼かたがた、先生のお宅を訪問した。Formal expression combining two purposes for a visit.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
ご挨拶{___}、上司の自宅に伺った。
(I called on my superior's home for the purpose of greetings and other matters.)
facts.explanation: かたがた (while/for dual purpose, formal) — ご挨拶かたがた、上司の自宅に伺った。Typical formal visit combining greeting with another errand.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
近況報告{___}、旧友に手紙を書いた。
(I wrote a letter to an old friend both to report on recent developments and for other purposes.)
facts.explanation: かたがた (while/for dual purpose, formal) — 近況報告かたがた、旧友に手紙を書いた。Written communication combining purposes.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
お詫び{___}、改めてご自宅に参りたいと思います。
(I would like to visit your home again both to apologize and for other purposes.)
facts.explanation: かたがた (while/for dual purpose, formal) — お詫びかたがた、改めてご自宅に参りたいと思います。Very formal apology visit context.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
努力する{___}、成功は得られない。
(Without making effort, success cannot be obtained.)
facts.explanation: ことなしに/ことなく (without doing) — 努力することなしに、成功は得られない。Negative condition: the main action cannot happen without the subordinate action.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
許可を得る{___}、この区域には入れない。
(Without obtaining permission, one cannot enter this area.)
facts.explanation: ことなしに/ことなく (without doing) — 許可を得ることなしに、この区域には入れない。Prerequisite that must be fulfilled.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
練習する{___}、上手になれるはずがない。
(Without practicing, there is no way one can improve.)
facts.explanation: ことなしに/ことなく (without doing) — 練習することなしに、上手になれるはずがない。Strong logical connection: improvement requires practice.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
犠牲を払う{___}、大きな成果は得られない。
(Without making sacrifices, great results cannot be obtained.)
facts.explanation: ことなしに/ことなく (without doing) — 犠牲を払うことなしに、大きな成果は得られない。Formal condition for achievement.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あなたの実力{___}、必ず合格できる。
(If using your abilities, you can certainly pass.)
facts.explanation: をもってすれば (if using/with) — あなたの実力をもってすれば、必ず合格できる。Expresses that with a certain means, something is achievable.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
現代の技術{___}、この問題も解決できるはずだ。
(If using modern technology, this problem should be solvable.)
facts.explanation: をもってすれば (if using/with) — 現代の技術をもってすれば、この問題も解決できるはずだ. Highlights a resource's capability.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼の知識{___}、どんな質問にも答えられる。
(With his knowledge, he can answer any question.)
facts.explanation: をもってすれば (if using/with) — 彼の知識をもってすれば、どんな質問にも答えられる. Emphasizes the sufficiency of a resource.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あのチームの力{___}、優勝は間違いない。
(With that team's strength, victory is certain.)
facts.explanation: をもってすれば (if using/with) — あのチームの力をもってすれば、優勝は間違いない. Confidence in the effectiveness of a means.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
信頼{___}、チームワークは成立しない。
(Without trust, teamwork cannot be established.)
facts.explanation: なくして(は) (without/if not for) — 信頼なくして、チームワークは成立しない。Strong logical condition: the main clause is impossible without the stated element.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
平和{___}、真の発展はあり得ない。
(Without peace, true development is impossible.)
facts.explanation: なくして(は) (without/if not for) — 平和なくして、真の発展はあり得ない。Often used in formal, philosophical statements.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
努力{___}、夢は実現できない。
(Without effort, dreams cannot be realized.)
facts.explanation: なくして(は) (without/if not for) — 努力なくして、夢は実現できない。Classic motivational use of this pattern.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
資金{___}、このプロジェクトは続けられない。
(Without funding, this project cannot continue.)
facts.explanation: なくして(は) (without/if not for) — 資金なくして、このプロジェクトは続けられない。Practical condition for continuation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
説明{___}、突然仕事を辞めた。
(He quit his job suddenly without any explanation.)
facts.explanation: なしに/なしで (without) — 説明なしに、突然仕事を辞めた。Used with nouns to indicate something is absent.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
断り{___}、部屋に入るのは失礼だ。
(It is rude to enter a room without permission.)
facts.explanation: なしに/なしで (without) — 断りなしに、部屋に入るのは失礼だ。Noun + なしに to indicate the absence of that thing.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
予告{___}、規則が変更された。
(The rules were changed without prior notice.)
facts.explanation: なしに/なしで (without) — 予告なしに、規則が変更された。Describes the absence of a notification before an action.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
理由{___}、彼は会議を欠席した。
(He was absent from the meeting without reason.)
facts.explanation: なしに/なしで (without) — 理由なしに、彼は会議を欠席した。Indicates absence of justification.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
現実{___}、計画を見直す必要がある。
(It is necessary to review the plan in line with reality.)
facts.explanation: に即して (in line with/conforming to) — 現実に即して、計画を見直す必要がある。Implies fitting or conforming to actual situations.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
時代の変化{___}、教育方針を改めた。
(The educational policy was revised in line with changes of the era.)
facts.explanation: に即して (in line with) — 時代の変化に即して、教育方針を改めた。Adjusting to conform to current situations.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
実情{___}、適切な支援を行うことが重要だ。
(It is important to provide appropriate support in line with the actual situation.)
facts.explanation: に即して (in line with) — 実情に即して、適切な支援を行うことが重要だ。Stresses the importance of fitting support to reality.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
学習者のレベル{___}、教材を選ぶべきだ。
(Teaching materials should be selected in line with the learner's level.)
facts.explanation: に即して (in line with) — 学習者のレベルに即して、教材を選ぶべきだ。Tailoring to fit the specific situation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
法律{___}、適切な手続きを踏む必要がある。
(It is necessary to follow the proper procedures in accordance with the law.)
facts.explanation: に則って (in accordance with) — 法律に則って、適切な手続きを踏む必要がある。Means strict adherence to rules, laws, or principles.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
校則{___}、制服を着なければならない。
(Students must wear uniforms in accordance with the school rules.)
facts.explanation: に則って (in accordance with) — 校則に則って、制服を着なければならない。Used when actions must follow established rules.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
伝統的な作法{___}、お茶を点てた。
(I prepared the tea in accordance with the traditional manner.)
facts.explanation: に則って (in accordance with) — 伝統的な作法に則って、お茶を点てた。Strong implication of following established tradition or rules.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
憲法{___}、政府は国民の権利を保護しなければならない。
(In accordance with the constitution, the government must protect citizens' rights.)
facts.explanation: に則って (in accordance with) — 憲法に則って、政府は国民の権利を保護しなければならない。Formal adherence to legal/constitutional frameworks.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
他国{___}、その技術を開発した。
(They developed that technology ahead of other countries.)
facts.explanation: に先駆けて (ahead of/preceding) — 他国に先駆けて、その技術を開発した。Doing something before others in a pioneering way.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
世界{___}、日本はその製品を市場に出した。
(Japan launched that product on the market ahead of the world.)
facts.explanation: に先駆けて (ahead of/preceding) — 世界に先駆けて、日本はその製品を市場に出した。Being the first in the world at something.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
発売{___}、プレス向けに試作品が公開された。
(Ahead of the release, prototypes were shown to the press.)
facts.explanation: に先駆けて (ahead of/preceding) — 発売に先駆けて、プレス向けに試作品が公開された。Actions done before a scheduled event.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
他の企業{___}、環境対策に取り組んだ。
(They tackled environmental measures ahead of other companies.)
facts.explanation: に先駆けて (ahead of/preceding) — 他の企業に先駆けて、環境対策に取り組んだ。Leadership or pioneering in a field.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
過去の事例{___}、今回の判断は適切だと言える。
(In light of past cases, it can be said that this decision was appropriate.)
facts.explanation: に照らして (in light of/when compared against) — 過去の事例に照らして、今回の判断は適切だと言える。Used for evaluation against a standard.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
法律の規定{___}、その行為は違法だ。
(In light of the legal provisions, that act is illegal.)
facts.explanation: に照らして (in light of) — 法律の規定に照らして、その行為は違法だ。Checking something against an established standard.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
一般常識{___}、その発言は問題がある。
(In light of common sense, that statement is problematic.)
facts.explanation: に照らして (in light of) — 一般常識に照らして、その発言は問題がある。Measuring behavior against common standards.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
自分の経験{___}、彼のアドバイスは的確だと思う。
(In light of my own experience, I think his advice is accurate.)
facts.explanation: に照らして (in light of) — 自分の経験に照らして、彼のアドバイスは的確だと思う。Using personal experience as the evaluative standard.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
地球の気温は年々上昇し{___}。
(The Earth's temperature is gradually rising year by year.)
facts.explanation: つつある (in the process of) — 地球の気温は年々上昇しつつある。Indicates an ongoing gradual change or process.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
少子化問題はますます深刻になり{___}。
(The declining birthrate problem is becoming increasingly serious.)
facts.explanation: つつある (in the process of) — 少子化問題はますます深刻になりつつある。Common with gradual societal changes.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
技術の進歩により、私たちの生活は変わり{___}。
(Due to technological advances, our lives are in the process of changing.)
facts.explanation: つつある (in the process of) — 技術の進歩により、私たちの生活は変わりつつある。Masu-stem + つつある.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
患者の容態は回復し{___}と医師は説明した。
(The doctor explained that the patient's condition is in the process of recovering.)
facts.explanation: つつある (in the process of) — 患者の容態は回復しつつあると医師は説明した。Used to describe ongoing recovery or improvement.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
長い訓練{___}、彼は一流の医師になった。
(After going through long training, he became a top-class doctor.)
facts.explanation: を経て (through/via/after passing through) — 長い訓練を経て、彼は一流の医師になった。Indicates passing through a process or place.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
数回の審議{___}、法案が可決された。
(After going through several rounds of deliberation, the bill was passed.)
facts.explanation: を経て (through/via) — 数回の審議を経て、法案が可決された。Common with formal processes or procedures.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
東京{___}、飛行機は大阪へ向かった。
(Via Tokyo, the airplane headed for Osaka.)
facts.explanation: を経て (through/via) — 東京を経て、飛行機は大阪へ向かった。Also used for physical routes or waypoints.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
多くの試練{___}、彼女は強くなった。
(Having gone through many trials, she became strong.)
facts.explanation: を経て (through/via) — 多くの試練を経て、彼女は強くなった。Used for experiences that lead to a result.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
友人の紹介{___}、彼女と知り合った。
(I got to know her through a friend's introduction.)
facts.explanation: を通じて (through/by means of) — 友人の紹介を通じて、彼女と知り合った。Indicates the means or medium through which something happens.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
インターネット{___}、世界中の情報を入手できる。
(Through the internet, you can obtain information from all over the world.)
facts.explanation: を通じて (through/by means of) — インターネットを通じて、世界中の情報を入手できる。Also can mean 'throughout a period': 一年を通じて (throughout the year).
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その会社は代理店{___}、製品を販売している。
(The company sells its products through agents.)
facts.explanation: を通じて (through/by means of) — その会社は代理店を通じて、製品を販売している。Marks the intermediary or channel.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
スポーツ{___}、国際交流を深めることができる。
(Through sports, international exchange can be deepened.)
facts.explanation: を通じて (through/by means of) — スポーツを通じて、国際交流を深めることができる. Marks the medium for achieving a goal.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
前回の結果{___}、今回の戦略を立てた。
(Based on the previous results, we formulated this time's strategy.)
facts.explanation: を踏まえて (based on/taking into account) — 前回の結果を踏まえて、今回の戦略を立てた。Using prior information as a foundation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
読者のニーズ{___}、内容を改善した。
(We improved the content based on readers' needs.)
facts.explanation: を踏まえて (based on/taking into account) — 読者のニーズを踏まえて、内容を改善した。Taking relevant data as the basis for decision.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
アンケートの回答{___}、サービスを見直した。
(We reviewed the service based on survey responses.)
facts.explanation: を踏まえて (based on/taking into account) — アンケートの回答を踏まえて、サービスを見直した。Common in business contexts.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
専門家の意見{___}、対策を講じた。
(We took measures based on experts' opinions.)
facts.explanation: を踏まえて (based on/taking into account) — 専門家の意見を踏まえて、対策を講じた。Expert opinion as the decision-making foundation.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
想像する{___}恐ろしい。
(It is terrifying even to imagine.)
facts.explanation: だに (even/just thinking about it) — 想像するだに恐ろしい。Literary/archaic particle meaning 'even (doing something minimal)'.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
考える{___}震えが止まらない。
(I can't stop trembling just thinking about it.)
facts.explanation: だに (even just) — 考えるだに震えが止まらない。Literary emphasis on minimal action triggering strong reaction.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
聞く{___}身の毛がよだつ話だった。
(It was a story that made your hair stand on end just hearing it.)
facts.explanation: だに (even just) — 聞くだに身の毛がよだつ話だった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
そんなことは夢{___}思わなかった。
(I never even dreamed such a thing could happen.)
facts.explanation: だに (even/not even in dreams) — 夢だに思わなかった。Fixed phrase: not even in one's dreams.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
疲れた{___}、お腹が痛い{___}と文句ばかり言っている。
(He keeps complaining — things like 'I'm tired' and 'my stomach hurts'.)
facts.explanation: だの～だの (listing complaints/examples) — 疲れただの、お腹が痛いだのと文句ばかり言っている。Used to list multiple examples, often disparagingly.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
高い{___}、遠い{___}と理由をつけて断った。
(He made excuses like 'it's expensive' and 'it's far away' and declined.)
facts.explanation: だの～だの (listing reasons/examples disparagingly) — 高いだの、遠いだのと理由をつけて断った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
甘い{___}、辛い{___}と好き嫌いが多い。
(He's so picky, saying things like 'it's too sweet' and 'it's too spicy'.)
facts.explanation: だの～だの (listing complaints) — 甘いだの、辛いだのと好き嫌いが多い。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
眠い{___}、退屈{___}と授業中ずっと不満を言っていた。
(He kept complaining throughout class, saying things like 'I'm sleepy' and 'I'm bored'.)
facts.explanation: だの～だの (listing complaints) — 眠いだの、退屈だのと不満を言っていた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
結果{___}、最善を尽くすことが重要だ。
(However the result turns out, giving your best is what matters.)
facts.explanation: はどうであれ (however it turns out/regardless) — 結果はどうであれ、最善を尽くすことが重要だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
動機{___}、行動には責任が伴う。
(Whatever the motive, actions carry responsibility.)
facts.explanation: はどうであれ (however it is) — 動機はどうであれ、行動には責任が伴う。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
理由{___}、約束を破ったことは許せない。
(Whatever the reason, breaking a promise is unforgivable.)
facts.explanation: はどうであれ (whatever it may be) — 理由はどうであれ、約束を破ったことは許せない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
過去{___}、今どう生きるかが大切だ。
(Whatever the past may have been, what matters is how you live now.)
facts.explanation: はどうであれ (however it was) — 過去はどうであれ、今どう生きるかが大切だ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
少し{___}力になれれば嬉しいです。
(I would be happy if I could help even a little.)
facts.explanation: なりとも (even a little/at least) — 少しなりとも力になれれば嬉しい。Expresses desire for even a minimal amount.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
一言{___}お礼が言いたかった。
(I wanted to say even just a word of thanks.)
facts.explanation: なりとも (even a little/just) — 一言なりともお礼が言いたかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
一時間{___}休みを取りたい。
(I want to take a rest, even if just for one hour.)
facts.explanation: なりとも (even just) — 一時間なりとも休みを取りたい。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
何{___}手伝えることがあれば言ってください。
(Please tell me if there is anything, even something small, I can help with.)
facts.explanation: なりとも (even a little) — 何なりとも手伝えることがあれば言ってください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は自分の名前{___}漢字で書けない。
(He cannot write even his own name in kanji.)
facts.explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 自分の名前すら漢字で書けない。Literary emphasis that the most basic thing is impossible.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
忙しすぎて、休む時間{___}ない。
(I am too busy — there is not even time to rest.)
facts.explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 休む時間すらない。Literary: even the minimal thing is absent.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
ベテランの選手{___}解けない問題だ。
(It is a problem that even veteran players cannot solve.)
facts.explanation: ですら (even) — ベテランの選手ですら解けない問題だ。ですら directly follows nouns.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は挨拶{___}せずに部屋を出て行った。
(She left the room without even saying hello.)
facts.explanation: すら (even) — 挨拶すらせずに部屋を出て行った。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
たとえ専門家{___}、この問題は難しい。
(Even experts would find this problem difficult.)
facts.explanation: とて (even/archaic) — たとえ専門家とて、この問題は難しい。Archaic/literary particle meaning 'even'.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
王様{___}法を破ることは許されない。
(Even a king is not permitted to break the law.)
facts.explanation: とて (even) — 王様とて法を破ることは許されない。Literary/classical register.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
神様{___}、運命を変えることはできない。
(Even God cannot change fate.)
facts.explanation: とて (even) — 神様とて運命を変えることはできない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
天才{___}、努力なしには成功できない。
(Even a genius cannot succeed without effort.)
facts.explanation: とて (even) — 天才とて、努力なしには成功できない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
うちの子供{___}、また宿題を忘れてきた。
(That child of mine — he forgot his homework again.)
facts.explanation: ったら (exasperation about someone) — うちの子供ったら、また宿題を忘れてきた。Expresses exasperation about a person's behavior.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あの人{___}、いつも遅刻するんだから。
(That person — always being late!)
facts.explanation: ったら (exasperation) — あの人ったら、いつも遅刻するんだから。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
もう、あなた{___}、いつもそうなんだから！
(Honestly, you — you're always like this!)
facts.explanation: ったら (exasperation) — あなたったら、いつもそうなんだから！
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
うちの夫{___}、片付けを全くしないんです。
(My husband — he absolutely never tidies up.)
facts.explanation: ったら (exasperation) — うちの夫ったら、片付けを全くしない。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
聞いてる{___}！何度言えばわかるの？
(I'm telling you, I heard it! How many times do I have to say it?)
facts.explanation: ってば (I'm telling you/emphatic) — 聞いてるってば！Expresses frustration that one is not being heard/believed.
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
もう行くな{___}、なんでついてくるの？
(I told you not to come, so why are you following me?)
facts.explanation: ってば (I'm telling you) — 行くなってば. Expresses repetition of instruction with exasperation.
